summary: Frontend testing for address form updates and admin listing flow settings route migration. Code implementation verified correct. Route redirect working. Found critical 403 bug on new listing-flow-settings route due to AdminRouteGuard timing issue.

ui_bugs.issue: 403 error when navigating to /admin/site-design/listing-flow-settings - AdminRouteGuard shows 403 because user object is null when guard renders even though token is present

integration_issues.issue: Direct navigation to /admin/site-design/listing-flow-settings shows 403 due to race condition in AuthContext user loading vs AdminRouteGuard check

action_items: Fix AdminRouteGuard timing issue - wait for AuthContext loading=false before checking user, or show loading state while user is being fetched
Enable address block in admin config to allow testing of user address form UI
critical_code_review_comments: Address form implementation is correct - new fields house_number, address_extra added with proper data-testid attributes
Route redirect from /admin/system-settings/listing-flow-settings to /admin/site-design/listing-flow-settings works correctly
Sidebar link for 'İlan Ver Akış Ayarları' correctly configured under Site İç Tasarımı section
System settings page correctly hides listing-create card when not on listing-flow-settings route
TR/DE label switching for address form fields correctly implemented (DE: Hausnummer/Adresszusatz/Postleitzahl, TR: Kapı No/Adres Ek Bilgisi/Posta Kodu)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Address form fields (house_number, address_extra) code implementation is correct but cannot be UI tested because admin config disables address block. The 403 error on /admin/site-design/listing-flow-settings is a critical bug - AdminRouteGuard checks user before AuthContext finishes loading.
rca_of_the_issue: The AdminRouteGuard at /app/frontend/src/components/AdminRouteGuard.js checks for user at line 15: 'if (!user)' and returns 403. However, when navigating directly to the protected route, AuthContext is still in loading state fetching user from /api/auth/me. The loading state is handled correctly at line 7-13, but if loading=false and user=null (race condition), the guard shows 403. Fix: Add check for token in localStorage when user is null and loading is false, or ensure AuthProvider properly awaits user fetch before setting loading=false.
